item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand. Thank you for your full honesty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Talking animals aren't nearly creative or interesting enough to merit an upvote. This leans too hard on trying to be cute, but something being adorable doesn't excuse the lack of originality, or shuffling off a ton of exposition into the dialogue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending was kinda cute, but most of the description sounds almost lifted wholesale from an event trigger in Stellaris, which deters me from upvoting it. I don't know, it's just sound too similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, if so, then I'll leave it. I'm glad you've enjoyed my SCP :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, I don't want to dictate your article over something as tiny as this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you :)

If you wish, I could remove the agent talking about how cute the mole is. I thought it would just be a funny thing to add, but if you find it to be a little bit iffy then I would be more than happy to remove it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What I like about this is that, unlike a lot of animal based articles I've seen recently, I actually care about these moles somewhat. You made a nice world here with the Mole NASA making this article pretty enjoyable.

My only nitpick is the agent calling the Mole cute and the censoring of the agents name because I am not a fan of redactions at all but something as tiny as that didn't ruin the article

+1

item.title: My first SCP!
item.content:encoded:
I would like to thank Uncle Nicolini for giving me a critique of this SCP. The image accompanying it is here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kret_mole.jpg

Edit: Oh, and how could I forget, Yormoongand, for helping me out with this as well. (Sorry)

Thank you all, and I hope you have a great read :)
